text.title: Puppeteer Stealth & Proxy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the puppeteer-stealth-proxy methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The worker MUST pick exactly one evasion stance: puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth alone, manual navigator overrides alone, or none. Combining plugin + manual overrides is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Stealth plugin changes navigator props the agent may later assert on; mixing with manual overrides produces contradictory state and brittle assertions.

rule.statement: The chosen stance MUST be documented in a top-of-file comment naming the stance and the rationale.
rule.rationale: Future agents read the file and need to know which stance is in effect before changing anything; undocumented stances drift.

rule.statement: Retries against rate-limit responses (429/403) MUST be bounded (max 2) and a circuit breaker MUST surface the error to the caller after threshold. Unbounded silent retries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unbounded retries burn budget fast and trigger IP/account bans; circuit-breaker keeps failures observable.

rule.statement: Proxies MUST be health-checked before use (HEAD request to a known endpoint) and unhealthy proxies MUST be removed from the pool until next refresh.
rule.rationale: Dead proxies cause silent stalls or HTTPS errors that look like target issues; health checks isolate.

rule.statement: Proxy credentials MUST be passed via page.authenticate({username,password}) — not via the --proxy-server=http://user:pass@host arg, which leaks credentials into the process command line.
rule.rationale: Command-line args appear in ps output and process telemetry; authenticate() keeps credentials in protected memory.

rule.statement: page.evaluate MUST NOT assert on navigator properties that the stealth stance overrides (webdriver, plugins, languages, hardwareConcurrency).
rule.rationale: Assertions on overridden props produce false negatives when the plugin/manual override changes; they should not be load-bearing.
